title: श्रीनाथजी स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री श्रीनाथजी स्तोत्रम् ॥

श्रीनाथं प्रणमामि गोकुलपतिं
गोवर्धनोद्धारिणम्।
गोपीनां हृदयैकवासमनिशं
गोपालमानन्ददम्॥ १॥

वामकरेण गिरिवरं धारयन्तं
दक्षिणकरं कटिनिहितं शोभमानम्।
नाथद्वारनिवासिनं गिरिधरं
श्रीनाथमीशं भजे॥ २॥

पुष्टिमार्गप्रवर्तकं वल्लभेशं
विट्ठलनाथतनयमाचार्यवर्यम्।
श्रीमद्वल्लभसम्प्रदायनिलयं
श्रीनाथमीशं भजे॥ ३॥

श्रृंगाररसमयं मुरलीधरं
गोपजनप्रियं नवनीतप्रियम्।
यमुनातटविहारिणं सुन्दरं
श्रीनाथमीशं भजे॥ ४॥

नाथद्वारे विराजमानं हरिं
भक्तवत्सलमनन्तकृपाकरम्।
सेवास्वरूपं प्रसन्नवदनं
श्रीनाथमीशं भजे॥ ५॥

अन्नकूटमहोत्सवप्रियं
गोपगोपीवल्लभं कृष्णरूपम्।
राजभोगनिवेदनप्रियं
श्रीनाथमीशं भजे॥ ६॥

श्रावणे मंगलस्नानशोभितं
जन्माष्टमीमहोत्सवविग्रहम्।
होलीरंगमहोत्सवोज्ज्वलं
श्रीनाथमीशं भजे॥ ७॥

श्रीनाथस्तोत्रमिदं शुभं
पठेद्भक्त्या तु यो नरः।
पुष्टिमार्गप्रसादेन
कृष्णप्रेमं लभेत् ध्रुवम्॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री श्रीनाथजी स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shrinathji Stotram ||

Shrinatham pranamami Gokula-patim
Govardhanoddarinam |
Gopinam hridaya-eka-vasam-anisham
Gopalam-anandadam || 1 ||

Vama-karena giri-varam dharayantam
Dakshina-karam kati-nihitam shobhamanam |
Nathdwara-nivasinam Giridharam
Shrinatham-isham bhaje || 2 ||

Pushti-marga-pravartakam Vallabhesham
Vitthalanatha-tanayam-acharya-varyam |
Shrimad-Vallabha-sampradaya-nilayam
Shrinatham-isham bhaje || 3 ||

Shringara-rasamayam Muralidharam
Gopa-jana-priyam navanita-priyam |
Yamuna-tata-viharinam sundaram
Shrinatham-isham bhaje || 4 ||

Nathdware virajamanam Harim
Bhakta-vatsalam-ananta-kripa-karam |
Seva-svarupam prasanna-vadanam
Shrinatham-isham bhaje || 5 ||

Annakuta-mahotsava-priyam
Gopa-Gopi-vallabham Krishna-rupam |
Raja-bhoga-nivedana-priyam
Shrinatham-isham bhaje || 6 ||

Shravane mangala-snana-shobhitam
Janmashtami-mahotsava-vigraham |
Holi-ranga-mahotsavoj-jvalam
Shrinatham-isham bhaje || 7 ||

Shrinatha-stotram-idam shubham
Pathed-bhaktya tu yo narah |
Pushti-marga-prasadena
Krishna-premam labhet dhruvam || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Shrinathji Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
गोकुल के पति, गोवर्धन उद्धारक, गोपियों के हृदय में एकमात्र निवास करने वाले, सदा आनन्द देने वाले गोपाल श्रीनाथ को प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ १॥

बाएँ हाथ से गिरिराज को धारण करते हुए, दाहिने हाथ को कटि पर रखे हुए शोभायमान, नाथद्वारा निवासी गिरिधर श्रीनाथ ईश्वर को भजता हूँ॥ २॥

पुष्टिमार्ग के प्रवर्तक, वल्लभेश, विट्ठलनाथ के पुत्र, श्रेष्ठ आचार्य, श्रीमद् वल्लभ सम्प्रदाय के आश्रय श्रीनाथ ईश्वर को भजता हूँ॥ ३॥

श्रृंगार रसमय, मुरलीधर, गोपजन प्रिय, मक्खन प्रिय, यमुना तट पर विहार करने वाले सुन्दर श्रीनाथ ईश्वर को भजता हूँ॥ ४॥

नाथद्वारा में विराजमान हरि, भक्तवत्सल, अनन्त कृपा करने वाले, सेवा स्वरूप, प्रसन्न वदन श्रीनाथ ईश्वर को भजता हूँ॥ ५॥

अन्नकूट महोत्सव प्रिय, गोप-गोपी वल्लभ, कृष्ण रूप, राजभोग निवेदन प्रिय श्रीनाथ ईश्वर को भजता हूँ॥ ६॥

श्रावण में मंगल स्नान से शोभित, जन्माष्टमी महोत्सव के विग्रह, होली रंग महोत्सव में उज्ज्वल श्रीनाथ ईश्वर को भजता हूँ॥ ७॥

जो मनुष्य भक्ति से इस शुभ श्रीनाथ स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है, वह पुष्टिमार्ग के प्रसाद से कृष्ण प्रेम अवश्य प्राप्त करता है॥ ८॥
benefits: Sacred prayer of the Nathdwara/Pushti Marg tradition
Invokes the blessing of krishna-prema through the grace of pushti marg
Traditionally associated with prosperity, devotion, and divine love
Ideal for devotees following Vallabhacharya Sampradaya
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles and grants fulfillment of desires
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Krishna, Shrinathji Stotram (श्रीनाथजी स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. This revered text originates from Pushti Marg / Vallabha Sampradaya. Regular recitation is believed to bestow Krishna-prema through the grace of Pushti Marg, invoke blessings of Shrinathji (Govardhan-dhari Krishna), and remove obstacles and grant fulfillment of desires. It is especially recommended on Wednesday (Budhvar), Friday (Shukravar), and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Janmashtami, Annakut, and Holi for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Shrinathji Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Shrinathji Stotram - prayer to Lord Shrinathji of Nathdwara in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Pushti Marg tradition.
relatedSearches: Govardhana-dhari Krishna seven year old form

seoFaqs.question: Who is Shrinathji and which Vaishnava tradition worships him?
seoFaqs.answer: Shrinathji is a manifestation of Krishna depicted as a seven-year-old child (balak) lifting Mount Govardhan with his upraised left arm to shelter the cowherds and cattle of Vraja from Indra's rains. He is the central presiding deity of the Pushti Marg, also called the Vallabh Sampradaya or Shuddhadvaita school of Vaishnavism, founded by Sri Vallabhacharya (1479-1531). The principal shrine is at Nathdwara in Rajasthan, 48 km north-east of Udaipur. The deity was originally worshipped on Govardhan Hill itself and was later moved to Nathdwara in 1672 to escape Mughal iconoclasm. The Wikipedia entry on Shrinathji and the Nathdwara Temple official site preserve this lineage history.

seoFaqs.question: Did Vallabhacharya compose a specific stotram for Shrinathji?
seoFaqs.answer: Vallabhacharya's most famous stotras within the Pushti Marg corpus are the Madhurashtakam (eight verses celebrating Krishna's all-pervading sweetness, beginning 'adharam madhuram vadanam madhuram'), the Yamunashtakam (eight verses to the Yamuna river), the Krishnashraya Stotra (refuge in Krishna), the Chatushloki (four-verse essence of Pushti Marg theology), and the Sanyasa Nirnaya. While Vallabhacharya did not compose a single stotra titled 'Shrinathji Stotram' in name, the Madhurashtakam is universally treated as the central Krishna-praise hymn of the Pushti Marg and is recited daily at every Shrinathji haveli. Modern devotional collections sometimes label later compilations of Pushti Marg verses as 'Shrinathji Stotram' for ease of reference.

seoFaqs.question: What is the iconographic significance of the Shrinathji form?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shrinathji svarupa is a self-manifested (svayambhu) form carved in black stone, depicting Krishna with his left arm raised to lift Govardhan, his right hand resting on his hip with a closed fist (concealing a butter ball or a diamond, depending on tradition), large lotus eyes, and a slight smile. Two cows and a serpent flank the deity. Each iconographic element points to a Pushti Marg theological concept: the upraised arm symbolises divine grace lifting the soul, the closed fist represents Krishna's hidden treasures bestowed only on the bhakta, the cows represent the souls under his protection, and the seven-year-old child form represents the maximum sweetness (madhurya) age in Krishna theology. Devotees see and serve this form, never the abstract divine, in keeping with the Pushti Marg seva framework.

seoFaqs.question: How is Shrinathji seva structured at Nathdwara?
seoFaqs.answer: Shrinathji is offered eight darshans (ashta-jhakhi) per day at Nathdwara, recreating the daily routine of Krishna as a child in Vraja: Mangla (early morning awakening), Shringar (adornment), Gwal (cowherd departure), Rajbhog (midday meal), Utthapan (afternoon awakening), Bhog (evening snack), Sandhya Arti (twilight worship) and Shayan (sleep preparation). Different Pushti Marg verses, kirtans and Haveli Sangeet ragas accompany each darshan, with the Ashtachhap poets (Surdas, Kumbhandas, Krishnadas, Paramanandadas, Govindswami, Chaturbhujdas, Chitswami and Nandadas) being the central poetic voices. Devotees who recite a 'Shrinathji Stotram' at home are typically participating in this larger seva framework even outside the temple.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Pushti Marg philosophy that frames Shrinathji worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Pushti Marg follows Vallabhacharya's Shuddhadvaita (pure non-dualism), which holds that Krishna is the supreme reality and the world is real and non-different from him, requiring no Mayavadin denial of phenomena. Liberation comes through Krishna's grace (pushti) rather than through knowledge or action alone. The soul does not seek merger but eternal seva (loving service) of Krishna in his divine play (lila). Shrinathji worship therefore emphasises emotional intimacy, child-Krishna affection (vatsalya bhava) and aesthetic delight (rasa) over austerity or speculative philosophy. Stotram recitation in this tradition is one mode of seva, alongside food offering, dressing the deity, music and visual contemplation, all aimed at participating in Krishna's continuous lila.

seoFaqs.question: When are Shrinathji-related stotras most importantly recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The most important annual occasion is Govardhan Puja and Annakut on the day after Diwali, when Shrinathji is offered a mountain of food (annakut) commemorating the lifting of Govardhan, and the entire Pushti Marg community recites Govardhan-praise verses through the day. Janmashtami (Krishna's birth), Nandotsav (the day after Janmashtami celebrating Nanda's joy), Holi, and the four monsoon months (Chaturmas) are other peak periods. Daily recitation accompanies the eight darshans, and Madhurashtakam in particular is sung at every haveli during Rajbhog. Householders who maintain a personal seva (ghar-mandir) at home perform abbreviated versions of these recitations daily, with special intensification during the month of Karthika and during personal festival days.
